name: The Roman aqueduct of Peña Cortada — Trail run circular
name_original: El acueducto romano de Peña Cortada — Trail run circular
region_intro: Jogging routes around Calles offer a diverse landscape for runners, characterized by its hilly terrain and historical features. The region features significant elevation changes, with routes often leading to scenic viewpoints and through areas with notable landmarks. Runners can expect a mix of natural paths and trails that highlight the area's unique geography. Best jogging routes around Calles The most popular jogging route is The Roman aqueduct of Peña Cortada — Trail run circular, a 7.5 miles (12.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 24 minutes to complete, featuring historical aqueduct views. Another top favourite among local runners is View of Calles – Decorative Fountain loop from Calles, a difficult 6.1 miles (9.8 km) path. This route offers panoramic views of Calles and passes by a decorative fountain. Local runners also love the Fuente de la Gitana – Descent from Pico del Remedio loop from Chelva, a 9.5 miles (15.3 km) trail leading through hilly terrain towards Pico del Remedio, often completed in about 2 hours 13 minutes. Jogging around Calles is defined by historical aqueducts, scenic viewpoints, and challenging ascents to local hills, with options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Calles are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from 4 reviews. More than 300 runners have used komoot to explore Calles's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Calles?
A: There are over 30 dedicated running routes around Calles, offering a diverse range of experiences for all levels of runners. The komoot community has explored these trails more than 300 times.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Calles?
A: Jogging routes around Calles are characterized by hilly terrain and historical features. You can expect significant elevation changes, with paths often leading to scenic viewpoints and through areas with notable landmarks. The routes offer a mix of natural paths and trails that highlight the area's unique geography, including challenging ascents to local hills.

Q: Are there any easy running routes for beginners in Calles?
A: While many routes in Calles are rated as difficult (22 out of 30), there is at least one easy route available. Additionally, 7 routes are considered moderate, providing options for those looking for less strenuous runs.

Q: What historical sights can I see while running in Calles?
A: The region is rich in historical features. One prominent example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e832528521/the-roman-aqueduct-of-pena-cortada-trail-run-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Roman aqueduct of Peña Cortada — Trail run circular&lt;/a&gt;, which offers impressive aqueduct views. You can also find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5437965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Cortada tunnels and galleries&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of the ancient aqueduct system.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Calles?
A: Yes, several routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e832528521/the-roman-aqueduct-of-pena-cortada-trail-run-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Roman aqueduct of Peña Cortada — Trail run circular&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14709960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Calles – Decorative Fountain loop from Calles&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I discover on the trails?
A: Beyond historical sites, the trails in Calles offer access to beautiful natural attractions. You can find highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5113553" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorros de Barchel Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1031861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Playeta de Chelva&lt;/a&gt; river area, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3028573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charco Azul of Chulilla&lt;/a&gt; lake. Many routes also feature scenic viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4579981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Frailecico Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that offer panoramic views of Calles?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14709960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Calles – Decorative Fountain loop from Calles&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice specifically for its panoramic views of the town. Many other routes, especially those with significant elevation gain, will also provide stunning vistas of the surrounding landscape.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Calles?
A: The running routes in Calles are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars from 4 reviews. Runners often praise the challenging ascents, the historical aqueduct views, and the diverse natural scenery.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by a decorative fountain?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14709960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Calles – Decorative Fountain loop from Calles&lt;/a&gt; specifically passes by a decorative fountain, adding a unique point of interest to your run.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to mountain peaks or viewpoints?
A: Many routes in Calles involve challenging ascents to local hills and peaks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10375731" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de la Gitana – Descent from Pico del Remedio loop from Chelva&lt;/a&gt; leads towards Pico del Remedio. You can also find specific viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4579981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Frailecico Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4049405" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Pico Frago&lt;/a&gt; along various trails.

Q: What is the typical duration for a running route in Calles?
A: The duration varies significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e832528521/the-roman-aqueduct-of-pena-cortada-trail-run-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Roman aqueduct of Peña Cortada — Trail run circular&lt;/a&gt; typically takes about 1 hour 24 minutes, while the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10375731" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de la Gitana – Descent from Pico del Remedio loop from Chelva&lt;/a&gt; often takes around 2 hours 13 minutes.
tips: Declared an Asset of Cultural Interest, this colossal work of hydraulic engineering was built by the Romans in the 1st century AD. The aqueduct is one of the most important in the country and conserves stretches of 28km.
The water route goes from Calles to Chelva along the Tuéjar river, it is perfect for a walk, it has no difficulty and there are areas with benches to rest

waypoints.name: Peña Cortada Aqueduct (Puente de la Rambla de Alcotas)
